text:
{
  "title":"CERTIFIED PUBLIC ACCOUNTANT FOUNDATION LEVEL 1 EXAMINATIONS F1.3: FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING",
   "description":"DATE: WEDNESDAY, 28 APRIL 2021",
   "timeLimit":195,
   "passingScore":0,
   "sections":[
      {
         "name":"A",
         "title":"SECTION A",
         "description":"This section has one compulsory question to be attempted.",
         "passage":"",
         "subsections":[],
         "questions":[
            {
               "text":"The following balances were extracted from the books of Kazubwenge manufacturers, a small manufacturing business for the year ended 31st December 2018.",
               "type":"financial-spreadsheet",
               "passage":"SN Accounts Dr Cr\n1 Sales - 18,676,800\n2 Raw material purchases 4,560,000 -\n3 Plant and equipment @ cost 5,536,000 -\n4 Motor vehicle @ cost 2,880,000 -\n5 Furniture and fittings @ cost 368,000 -\n6 Cash at Bank 108,000 -\n7 Accounts receivable 1,840,000 -\n8 Drawings 1,200,000 -\n9 Motor vehicle (used by salesmen) 2,880,000 -\n10 Capital - 6,486,400\n11 Accounts payable - 1,720,000\n12 Administration expenses 3,007,200 -\n13 Selling and distribution expenses 1,328,000 -\n14 Advertising expenses 240,000 -\n15 Factory direct wages 1,200,000 -\n16 Factory indirect wages 480,000 -\n17 Factory power 720,000 -\n18 Heat and light 320,000 -\n19 Carriage inwards 80,000 -\n20 Rent & rates 400,000 -\n21 Return outwards - 264,000\nTotal 27,147,200 27,147,200",
               "points":40,
               "subQuestions":[
                  {
                     "label":"a",
                     "text":"Prepare for Kazubwenge Ltd a statement of Manufacturing Account.",
                     "type":"financial-spreadsheet",
                     "points":19,
                     "spreadsheetTemplate":"{}",
                     "spreadsheetModelAnswer":"{}"
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"b",
                     "text":"Prepare for Kazubwenge Ltd a statement of Apportionment of shared costs.",
                     "type":"financial-spreadsheet",
                     "points":6,
                     "spreadsheetTemplate":"{}",
                     "spreadsheetModelAnswer":"{}"
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"c",
                     "text":"Prepare for Kazubwenge Ltd a statement of Profit or loss and other comprehensive income.",
                     "type":"financial-spreadsheet",
                     "points":15,
                     "spreadsheetTemplate":"{}",
                     "spreadsheetModelAnswer":"{}"
                  }
               ],
               "correctAnswer":""
            }
         ]
      },
      {
         "name":"B",
         "title":"SECTION B",
         "description":"This section has four questions, three questions to be attempted.",
         "passage":"",
         "subsections":[],
         "questions":[
            {
               "text":"As the Manager of Twihute SACCO, you have been invited to attend the board meeting which is evaluating the financial performance of the cooperative for the year ended December 2019.",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"Statement of Financial Position as at 31 December 2019\nItem Dec-19 Dec-18\nCash in vault 9,196,215 11,035,458\nCash in Banks and other FIs 140,190,230 182,247,299\nGross Loans 112,449,961 84,337,472\nNon-current assets 58,152,143 50,592,364\nReceivables 159,714,623 117,787,874\nTotal assets 479,703,172 446,000,467\n5 years Borrowing from VUP 57,020,000 70,704,800\nTotal deposits 277,471,926 188,680,910\nOther long-term payables 11,212,100 69,102,296\nTotal Liabilities 345,704,026 328,488,006\nPaid up capital 42,560,750 42,560,750\nRetained Profit/Ac Losses 70,359,329 55,348,179\nProfit/Loss for the period 21,079,067 19,603,532\nTotal Equity 133,999,146 117,512,461\nTotal Equity and Liabilities 479,703,172 446,000,467",
               "points":20,
               "subQuestions":[
                  {
                     "label":"a",
                     "text":"Compute the following ratios to support your intervention in the board meeting.",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "points":12,
                     "subQuestions":[
                        {
                           "label":"i",
                           "text":"Net profit and Return on capital employed ratios to show the Profitability of the SACCO",
                           "type":"open-ended",
                           "points":0,
                           "correctAnswer":""
                        },
                        {
                           "label":"ii",
                           "text":"Liquidity ratio (Current)",
                           "type":"open-ended",
                           "points":0,
                           "correctAnswer":""
                        },
                        {
                           "label":"iii",
                           "text":"Non-performing loan ratio",
                           "type":"open-ended",
                           "points":0,
                           "correctAnswer":""
                        }
                     ],
                     "correctAnswer":""
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"b",
                     "text":"Draft a report to the chairman of the board explaining the financial performance and advise him whether a bonus should be paid.",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "points":8,
                     "correctAnswer":""
                  }
               ],
               "correctAnswer":""
            },
            {
               "text":"Write short notes on the following:",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "points":20,
               "subQuestions":[
                  {
                     "label":"i",
                     "text":"Three users of accounting information and the main interest to such information.",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "points":3,
                     "correctAnswer":""
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"ii",
                     "text":"Three enhancing qualitative characteristics of financial information.",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "points":3,
                     "correctAnswer":""
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"iii",
                     "text":"Four ethical principles which should guide the conducts of a professional accountant.",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "points":4,
                     "correctAnswer":""
                  }
               ],
               "correctAnswer":""
            },
            {
               "text":"Inyarwanda co. is a private limited company dealing in bakery.",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"Issue 1: the company has huge amounts of stock as indicated in the table below but the accountant was confused with the price to use while computing the closing balance.\nSN item Quantity Cost price Selling Price Market price\n1 Bread 12,500 800 1200 1000\n2 Cakes 620 900 1500 700",
               "points":20,
               "subQuestions":[
                  {
                     "label":"Issue 1",
                     "text":"Compute the value of the inventory to be disclosed in the balance sheet.",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "points":4,
                     "correctAnswer":""
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"Issue 2",
                     "text":"Explain the recognition conditions which should be met before such grant is recognized.",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "points":3,
                     "correctAnswer":""
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"Issue 3",
                     "text":"Discuss the expenditures related to research and development costs.",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "points":3,
                     "correctAnswer":""
                  }
               ],
               "correctAnswer":""
            },
            {
               "text":"Inyenyeri limited has an accounting year ending 30 June.",
               "type":"financial-spreadsheet",
               "passage":"SN Accounts Dr Cr\n1 Building at cost 14,800,000 -\n2 Building accumulated depreciation, 1 July 2019 - 1,200,000\n3 Plant at cost 4,400,000 -\n4 Plant accumulated depreciation, 1 July 2019 - 2,200,000\n5 Bank balance - 1,400,000\n6 Sales - 36,000,000\n7 Purchases 22,800,000 -\n8 Inventory at 1 July 2019 3,200,000 -\n9 Cash 400,000 -\n10 Trade receivables 6,400,000 -\n11 Trade payables - 5,000,000\n12 Allowance for receivables - 200,000\n13 Office expenses 6,500,000 -\n14 Insurance 300,000 -\n15 Discount allowed 150,000 -\n16 Discount received - 400,000\n17 Return inwards 250,000 -\n18 Return outwards - 300,000\n19 Retained Earnings at 1 July 2019 - 2,600,000\n20 Equity share Frw 5000 - 8,300,000\n21 Share Premium - 1,600,000\nTotal 59,200,000 59,200,000",
               "points":20,
               "spreadsheetTemplate":"{}",
               "spreadsheetModelAnswer":"{}",
               "correctAnswer":""
            },
            {
               "text":"Bucyana and Rugira, trading partnership, sharing the balance of profits and losses in the proportions 5:3 respectively.",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"Accounts Amount \"Frw\"\nSales 16,605,000\nStock on 1st October 2019 1,150,000\nPurchases 9,410,000\nHeat and light 205,000\nStaff salaries 1,600,000\nTelephone charges 145,000\nMotor vehicle running expenses 281,000\nReturn outwards 102,500\nReceivables 415,750\nPayables 135,000\nDiscounts receivable 18,500\nCarriage inwards 85,000\nFixtures and fittings at cost 1,300,000\nMotor vehicles at cost 2,300,000\nBalance at Bank 898,250\nCurrent accounts credit balances\nAt 1st October 2019\nBucyana 180,000\nRugira 120,000\nCapital account balances\nOn 1 October 2019:\nBucyana: 1,650,000\nRugira: 850,000",
               "points":20,
               "subQuestions":[
                  {
                     "label":"i",
                     "text":"Prepare a profit or loss appropriation account for the year ended 30 September 2020.",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "points":10,
                     "correctAnswer":""
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"ii",
                     "text":"Prepare a balance sheet as of 30 September 2020 which should include summaries of the partners' capital and current accounts for the year ended on that date.",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "points":8,
                     "correctAnswer":""
                  }
               ],
               "correctAnswer":""
            }
         ]
      }
   ]
}

sections.title: SECTION A
sections.description: This section has one compulsory question to be attempted.

questions.text: The following balances were extracted from the books of Kazubwenge manufacturers, a small manufacturing business for the year ended 31st December 2018.
questions.passage:
SN Accounts Dr Cr
1 Sales - 18,676,800
2 Raw material purchases 4,560,000 -

4 Motor vehicle @ cost 2,880,000 -
5 Furniture and fittings @ cost 368,000 -

7 Accounts receivable 1,840,000 -
8 Drawings 1,200,000 -
9 Motor vehicle (used by salesmen) 2,880,000 -
10 Capital - 6,486,400
11 Accounts payable - 1,720,000
12 Administration expenses 3,007,200 -
13 Selling and distribution expenses 1,328,000 -
14 Advertising expenses 240,000 -
15 Factory direct wages 1,200,000 -
16 Factory indirect wages 480,000 -
17 Factory power 720,000 -

19 Carriage inwards 80,000 -
20 Rent & rates 400,000 -
21 Return outwards - 264,000
Total 27,147,200 27,147,200

subQuestions.label: a
subQuestions.text: Prepare for Kazubwenge Ltd a statement of Manufacturing Account.

subQuestions.label: b
subQuestions.text: Prepare for Kazubwenge Ltd a statement of Apportionment of shared costs.

subQuestions.label: c
subQuestions.text: Prepare for Kazubwenge Ltd a statement of Profit or loss and other comprehensive income.

sections.name: B
sections.title: SECTION B
sections.description: This section has four questions, three questions to be attempted.

questions.text: As the Manager of Twihute SACCO, you have been invited to attend the board meeting which is evaluating the financial performance of the cooperative for the year ended December 2019.
questions.passage:
Statement of Financial Position as at 31 December 2019
Item Dec-19 Dec-18
Cash in vault 9,196,215 11,035,458
Cash in Banks and other FIs 140,190,230 182,247,299
Gross Loans 112,449,961 84,337,472

5 years Borrowing from VUP 57,020,000 70,704,800

Other long-term payables 11,212,100 69,102,296
Total Liabilities 345,704,026 328,488,006
Paid up capital 42,560,750 42,560,750
Retained Profit/Ac Losses 70,359,329 55,348,179
Profit/Loss for the period 21,079,067 19,603,532
Total Equity 133,999,146 117,512,461
Total Equity and Liabilities 479,703,172 446,000,467

subQuestions.label: a
subQuestions.text: Compute the following ratios to support your intervention in the board meeting.

subQuestions.label: i
subQuestions.text: Net profit and Return on capital employed ratios to show the Profitability of the SACCO

subQuestions.label: b
subQuestions.text: Draft a report to the chairman of the board explaining the financial performance and advise him whether a bonus should be paid.

questions.text: Write short notes on the following:

subQuestions.label: i
subQuestions.text: Three users of accounting information and the main interest to such information.

subQuestions.label: ii
subQuestions.text: Three enhancing qualitative characteristics of financial information.

subQuestions.label: iii
subQuestions.text: Four ethical principles which should guide the conducts of a professional accountant.

questions.text: Inyarwanda co. is a private limited company dealing in bakery.
questions.passage:
Issue 1: the company has huge amounts of stock as indicated in the table below but the accountant was confused with the price to use while computing the closing balance.
SN item Quantity Cost price Selling Price Market price
1 Bread 12,500 800 1200 1000
2 Cakes 620 900 1500 700

subQuestions.label: Issue 1
subQuestions.text: Compute the value of the inventory to be disclosed in the balance sheet.

subQuestions.label: Issue 2
subQuestions.text: Explain the recognition conditions which should be met before such grant is recognized.

subQuestions.label: Issue 3
subQuestions.text: Discuss the expenditures related to research and development costs.
